criteria: Translate package.json to pyproject.toml (or setup.py) with correct dependencies (click, python-frontmatter) and proper entry point configuration for `python3 -m mdforge`.

Map JavaScript's src/ directory with index.js barrel exports to Python packages with __init__.py files — mdforge/parser/__init__.py, mdforge/transform/__init__.py, mdforge/validator/__init__.py, mdforge/utils/__init__.py, mdforge/config/__init__.py.
CommonJS require/module.exports correctly translated to Python import/from statements with relative imports within packages.
MdForgeError custom exception class preserves exit_code attribute. Exit code 0 for success, 1 for general errors (file not found, invalid input), 2 for validation failures with broken links.
Top-level error boundary in entry point catches MdForgeError and calls sys.exit(err.exit_code). Unexpected errors exit with code 1.
Commander.js replaced with Click. All six subcommands (parse, toc, html, stats, validate, batch) registered with correct arguments and options matching the source CLI surface.

gray-matter replaced with python-frontmatter. Frontmatter parsing returns equivalent data structure — empty dict when no frontmatter present, full YAML parse when present, graceful fallback on malformed YAML.
npm glob package replaced with pathlib.Path.rglob or stdlib glob. Recursive Markdown file discovery produces sorted absolute paths matching source behavior.
Chalk removed. Logging uses ANSI escape codes or no color. All log messages go to stderr, all CLI output (JSON, HTML, TOC) goes to stdout.

parse command produces JSON output with correct keys: file, frontmatter, headings (with level, text, slug, line), links (with text, url, type, line), codeBlocks (with language, code, startLine, endLine), wordCount.
toc command generates Markdown TOC: h1 excluded, h2 at base indent, h3 indented 2 spaces, h4 indented 4 spaces. Each entry is a Markdown link with correct slug anchor.

html command converts Markdown to simplified HTML matching source behavior exactly: headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks with language class, links, unordered lists, ordered lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, paragraphs. Frontmatter stripped from output.

stats command produces JSON with aggregate statistics: fileCount, totalWordCount, totalLineCount, totalHeadings, totalLinks, totalCodeBlocks, averageWordCount, files array, codeLanguages distribution.

validate command checks internal and anchor links across a corpus. Returns JSON with valid boolean, totalChecked, validCount, brokenCount, brokenLinks array. Exits 0 when all valid, exits 2 when broken links found. External and mailto links are not validated.

batch command processes directory of Markdown files, writes one JSON file per input to output directory. JSON contains metadata/frontmatter, headings, links, stats, toc. Reports processed/failed counts in stdout JSON.
Slug generation algorithm matches source exactly: lowercase, strip non-word chars (except hyphens), spaces to hyphens, collapse multiple hyphens, trim leading/trailing hyphens.
Headings inside fenced code blocks are NOT extracted. Links inside fenced code blocks are NOT extracted. Code block fence matching handles both backtick and tilde fences.
Word count strips fenced code blocks and inline code before counting whitespace-delimited tokens. Empty content returns 0.
Unclosed fenced code blocks are discarded — no codeBlock entry produced. Content inside unclosed fence is consumed (headings/links not extracted from it).

Python code uses snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes. pathlib.Path used for file operations. List comprehensions used instead of filter/map. Context managers used where appropriate.
JSON output keys remain camelCase to match source CLI output exactly (codeBlocks, wordCount, lineCount, startLine, endLine, etc.) even though internal Python variables use snake_case.
Async removed — synchronous I/O used throughout. No asyncio, no aiofiles, no async def in the translated code. This is the idiomatic Python approach for a CLI tool.
Entry point configured so that both `python3 /target/bin/mdforge.py <cmd>` and `python3 -m mdforge <cmd>` work correctly after pip install -e .
